reasoning: The calculated threshold requires over 105 distinct protest events within a 30-day period across El Salvador (compared to an average of ~9.5/month in the preceding year). El Salvador typically sees around 1 protest every 3 days. Experiencing 3-4 protests every single day for a month would require an unprecedented nationwide mobilization. The current political climate heavily depresses protest capacity: President Nayib Bukele maintains very high approval ratings and enforces an ongoing "State of Exception" that suspends several constitutional rights, including freedom of assembly. Mass gatherings and protests are actively discouraged and dispersed. Current news from early 2025 shows only minor, localized demonstrations regarding anti-gang policies and the lifting of a mining ban. May Day may lead to a minor spike, but nothing near the 100+ events required. Consequently, the probability of exceeding this 10x threshold is exceptionally low across all timeframes, though marginally higher for later dates due to inherent forecasting uncertainty.
